questions.description: Bernie died suddenly from a short illness in January 2015. Advise John and Mary.
questions.text: Bernie died suddenly from a short illness in January 2015. At the time of her death, she was living on the family farm with her husband Frederick (which she had inherited some 20 years previous). In her will, Bernie left all of her worldly belongings to her son, John. Bernie also has a daughter, Mary, from a previous relationship to whom she left nothing under the will. Upon discovering her mother's death, Mary claims she was left out of the will as she did not get along with her mother. However, she does acknowledge that her mother gave her €15,000 10 years ago to start up a new business venture which never took off. In fact, Mary was challenged by alcohol and drug addiction at the time and mishandled the money as a result. Mary has been in recovery for the past five years and is currently unemployed and living in local authority housing. John, on the other hand, is married with three children and is a wealthy dentist. Advise John and Mary.

questions.description: According to the Property Registration Authority of Ireland, By international comparison, Ireland has a very extensive…
questions.text: According to the Property Registration Authority of Ireland, By international comparison, Ireland has a very extensive and well developed system of land registration. Since the foundation of the Land Registry in 1892, there has been a gradual, ongoing and continuous programme of movement away from the older and limited system of recording deeds (in the Registry of Deeds), to the more modern, flexible and comprehensive 'title registration' system provided through the Land Registry. 93% of the total land mass of the State and almost 90% of the legal titles in Ireland are now registered in the Land Registry. In light of the above statement, outline and critically assess the system for recording property transactions in relation to property in Ireland.

questions.description: In 2000, Tom, a farmer, sold one of his fields to Jigsaw Developments Ltd. Advise Jigsaw Developments Ltd.
questions.text: In 2000, Tom, a farmer, sold one of his fields to Jigsaw Developments Ltd. The field was adjacent to a main road which the local authorities had indicated would be upgraded to motorway status in the near future. Jigsaw Developments Ltd bought the property from Tom with the intention of building a large motorway service station on the site. Once the sale of the land was complete, Jigsaw Developments Ltd wrote a letter to Tom asking him if he could remove his sheep from the field and requesting him to place one of their advertising signs on the gate nearest the road. However, Tom, being a fairly laid back type of person, left these tasks on the long finger. In fact, due to various delays in the motorway development, Tom continued to graze his sheep on the land for a number of years. In 2004, Tom decided that a change was needed and he left farming. He sold his sheep and retired to the Canary Islands. Before he left, he made an agreement to lease the field to his neighbour, Joe, so that he could grow some mushrooms. Joe erected a number of structures in the field to serve as growing rooms. In 2007, the Chief Executive of Jigsaw Developments Ltd was driving by the field and noticed that there was some kind of funny looking structure in it. He parked the car and looked in over the gate at the structure in the field. He decided to notify his solicitor who wrote to Joe requesting that he leave this private property immediately. Joe replied and said he had intended on leaving anyway as he was getting tired of the mushroom farming and in any case, more traffic on a motorway would ruin his crops. At the beginning of this year, Jigsaw Developments Ltd has finally started on the motorway development but Joe has decided he wants to remain on the land to try and grow more organic vegetables. Advise Jigsaw Developments Ltd.

questions.description: Critically analyse, with reference to case law, the approach of the Irish Courts to the admissibility of extrinsic…
questions.text: Critically analyse, with reference to case law, the approach of the Irish Courts to the admissibility of extrinsic evidence in the construction of wills.

questions.description: Fred has issued a new lease to George for cellars which lie below and adjacent to property retained by him.
questions.text: Fred has issued a new lease to George for cellars which lie below and adjacent to property retained by him. The lease agreement specifically states that the cellars will be used for a new tapas bar. However, upon inspection of these cellars, the local Health Authority has stated that the ventilation system which is in place is wholly inadequate and a new ventilation system will have to be installed. George has acted accordingly and hired a company to install the new system which involves fixing new piping to the property that has been retained by the landlord. Fred immediately questions George's right to do this and now seeks your advice. Discuss with reference to relevant case law.

questions.description: Discuss [any two]{.underline} of the following areas of land law: (a) Rights of residence (b) Estoppel licences (c) The…
questions.text: Discuss [any two]{.underline} of the following areas of land law: (a) Rights of residence (b) Estoppel licences (c) The influence of equity in Irish Land Law

questions.description: Under Landlord and Tenant Law, a landlord cannot unreasonably withhold his consent to a change of use or alienation.
questions.text: Under Landlord and Tenant Law, a landlord cannot unreasonably withhold his consent to a change of use or alienation. Discuss.

questions.description: After a night out, Martin decided to take a short cut home through a meadow which was beside his house.
questions.text: After a night out, Martin decided to take a short cut home through a meadow which was beside his house. While trespassing on this property, he tripped over something which was half buried in the soil. Upon further investigation, he realised that he had discovered what appeared to be a very valuable silver chalice. Margaret, who had taken out a ninety-nine year lease of the meadow the previous year, claimed that she should be entitled to the find. As word got around the local village, Margaret's landlord, Tim, heard about the find and claimed that since he owned the field, he should be entitled to the chalice. However, Martin now claims that since he was the one to discover the chalice, he should be entitled to keep it. Discuss.

questions.description: "In determining whether the possession of land is adverse for the purposes of the Statute of Limitations, the…
questions.text: "In determining whether the possession of land is adverse for the purposes of the Statute of Limitations, the intentions of the legal owner are irrelevant the only intention which matters is that of the squatter." Critically analyse this statement, supporting your answer with relevant legal authority .──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

questions.description: In October 2008, Monica had to go into hospital for a serious heart… Advise Mike and Cheryl.
questions.text: In October 2008, Monica had to go into hospital for a serious heart operation. She decided that since there were so many risks involved that she should make a will in case anything went wrong. Monica was quite nervous at the thought of the operation so the nurse put her under light sedation. In the meantime, her solicitor Joey came into the hospital to help with drawing up the will. Since Monica was feeling a bit drowsy, she asked Joey to sign the bottom of the will for her. Joey did so with Monica's name and also signed his own name. He then asked the doctor and nurse who were tending to the patient in the bed next to Monica's to also sign. Neither the doctor or the nurse looked at the document they just signed it as they were engaged in deep conversation. Monica left all her worldly belongings to her husband Mike (worth approximately €500,000). She made no provision for her daughter, Cheryl, as she felt that she was old enough to look after herself now and besides, she had supported her for years while in University. In August 2010, Monica discovered that Mike was having an affair with his secretary and threw him out of the house. She phoned Joey, her solicitor, and asked him to destroy the will she made in 2008 and rewrite a new one, leaving her entire estate to the cats and dogs home. Following the phone call, Joey tore up the will and left it in a bag for shredding. He then drew up the new will, as requested, for Monica. However, that evening, Monica was knocked down and killed while walking the dog. She had not signed the new will. Mike and Cheryl both claim that they should be entitled to something from Monica's estate. Advise Mike and Cheryl.

questions.description: Critically evaluate the creation and effect of rights of way and the right to light and the right to support, making…
questions.text: Critically evaluate the creation and effect of rights of way and the right to light and the right to support, making suggestions for reform.

questions.description: In May 2016, Breda went on holidays in the West of Ireland. Advise Breda.
questions.text: In May 2016, Breda went on holidays in the West of Ireland. One afternoon, she took her dog for a walk in what she believed was a private garden owned by the B & B in which she was staying. While strolling along, she stumbled on a large gold object which was covered in soil from the ground. Breda was always of the belief that "finders keepers, losers weepers". However, since she has always considered herself to be an honest person, she decided to tell the owner of the B & B, Kenneth. Kenneth informed Breda that the garden doesn't actually belong to him but to his sister Julia, who took out a 99-year lease on the land a couple of years ago. Kenneth has suggested that perhaps his sister is entitled to it. Advise Breda.

questions.description: Critically analyse, with reference to relevant case law, the approach of the Irish Courts to the admissibility of…
questions.text: Critically analyse, with reference to relevant case law, the approach of the Irish Courts to the admissibility of extrinsic evidence in the construction of wills.

questions.description: Jack successfully applied for a mortgage in 2009 so that he could build an… Mortgage.
questions.text: Jack successfully applied for a mortgage in 2009 so that he could build an extension onto his shop premises in a small village in Co. Mayo. Over the past few years, Jack used up all of his savings to cover his mortgage repayments in the absence of any profit being generated from his business. Unfortunately over the past year, he has started to fall into arrears on his mortgage repayments. Advise the bank what its options are in terms of enforcing its security of the loan to Jack. Refer to legal and policy developments in your answer.

questions.description: Critically analyse the legislative protection afforded to spouses and same sex couples with regard to family property…
questions.text: Critically analyse the legislative protection afforded to spouses and same sex couples with regard to family property in Ireland. Support your answer with relevant legal authority.

questions.description: (ANSWER (A) AND (B)) (a) Stewie and Brian were joint tenants of a house in… Advise the respective executors as to who…
questions.text: (ANSWER (A) AND (B)) (a) Stewie and Brian were joint tenants of a house in the Cork suburbs. Last year, they went on a holiday during which they took a trip together in a hot air balloon. Both were killed at the same time when the balloon in which they were both tcaveiåing struck a mountain. By his will, Stewie left all of his property to his sister, Meg. By his will, Brian ten all of his property to his children Peter and Lois. Advise the respective executors as to who is entitled to the house. And (b) Tom and Gerry were in business together as solicitors and, in 2005, decided to purchase a propety in Meath to accommodate their practice. Each put €500,000 towards the purchase price of €1,000,000 and the property was conveyed uto Tom and Gerry". Relations have recently become strained between the two of them and Gerry now wishes to set up his own practice in Dublin. He wants to force a sale of the property or to make Tom pay him a rent if Tom continues to occupy the premises. Gerry has recently had a heart bypass operation and is concerned about whether his family would have a claim to the property if he happened to die before matters are resolved. Advise the parties. ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
